item.title: Neo-Nazi leader Thomas Sewell covertly broadcasting hate on Instagram from Melbourne acreage
item.description: ABC NEWS Verify can reveal that Neo-Nazi leader Thomas Sewell has been uploading antisemitic and homophobic diatribes to Instagram from a property on the outskirts of Melbourne.

item.title: Sydney's 2SER community radio to avoid closure under reduced model
item.description: The 46-year-old Sydney community radio institution was facing closure after around half its core funding was withdrawn late last year by Macquarie University.

item.title: People with intellectual disability continue to receive substandard healthcare as program to train doctors risks closure
item.description: Sam Stubbs’s parents were asked about his quality of life while treating him for a lung infection. They realised it was because he was born with Down syndrome.

item.title: Missing Tasmanian devil will likely survive as Gold Coast search goes into second day
item.description: Sniffer dogs and thermal imaging drones are being deployed as the search for a missing Tasmanian devil on the Gold Coast goes into a second day.

item.title: Water Corp defends response to leaks and sewage spills amid flood of complaints
item.description: For two years, residents in one of the driest parts of Western Australia have watched thousands of litres of water leaking from a Water Corp-owned tank.

item.title: Ali Brigginshaw steps down as Broncos NRLW skipper, opens up on Gorden Tallis drama
item.description: Ali Brigginshaw relinquishes the Broncos captaincy in her final NRLW season and reveals her view about a club meeting room being renamed in her honour.

item.title: Gold reserves at Kalgoorlie Super Pit continue to grow with millions spent on underground drilling
item.description: A big exploration push by Australia's biggest gold miner, Northern Star Resources, has grown the reserves at the Super Pit by 54 per cent in the past six years.

item.title: ABS estimates 80pc of tobacco consumed in Australia last year illegal amid 'rapid growth' in black market
item.description: The experimental estimates are based on "nicotine metabolite concentrations" detected in wastewater samples, combined with data showing a collapse in spending on legal cigarettes and vapes.

item.title: Can one optimal pathway have two lanes? When it comes to AUKUS submarines, apparently yes
item.description: Scrutiny of AUKUS's delivery reveals two "optimal pathways" for Australia's submarines.

item.title: The bushland where a Tasmanian Devil is believed to be missing
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Police allege corporate boss refused blood alcohol tests after Sydney car crash
item.description: Private equity baron Joel Thickins is alleged to have crashed his BMW into five cars in Sydney's east, before refusing two alcohol-detecting breath tests.

item.title: Sydney Morning Herald takes down opinion piece about AI after author used AI to write it
item.description: The Sydney Morning Herald has taken down an opinion piece defending the use of artificial intelligence at universities, after it emerged its author used AI to write it.

item.title: Alice Springs's Tangentyere CEO Walter Shaw defends state of town camp housing
item.description: After the death of Kumanjayi Little Baby, the state of Alice Springs's town camps shocked the nation. Now, the man in charge of servicing them is speaking out.

item.title: Man dies in custody after crashing ute into Mudgee police station
item.description: A man has died in a maximum security prison days after driving his car at police officers, narrowly missing them, before crashing into a police station in Western New South Wales.

item.title: Wednesday Finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Australian Federal Police intercept luxury yacht, allege three people try to help Queensland fugitive
item.description: WA Police and Australian Federal Police intercepted a yacht off the coast of Exmouth in northern WA which allegedly had a Queensland fugitive on board.

item.title: Childcare worker Edwina Ling sentenced to four years' jail for assaulting toddlers
item.description: Queensland childcare worker Edwina Ling has been jailed for four years after pleading guilty to more than 80 counts of assault involving three young children.

item.title: Ukraine drones hit St Petersburg, while NATO chief makes unannounced Kyiv visit
item.description: An oil export terminal was hit hours before the Russian president's annual economic forum got underway.

item.title: Australia faces proposed 12.5pc US tariff over forced labour crackdown
item.description: Australia could be hit with a new US tariff after the Trump administration accused dozens of countries of failing to crack down on goods made with forced labour.

item.title: Former head of Indonesian free school meals program arrested by attorney-general
item.description: The ex-chief of Indonesia's free school meals program linked to food poisoning cases has been arrested after a corruption probe.

item.title: AI may be tightening jobs market, but some roles could see stronger demand
item.description: A new economic report by consultancy firm Deloitte says the AI "winners" will be organisations that combine human and machine strengths, with recruiters saying human judgement will still be key.

item.title: Trump says he was 'perturbed' during expletive-laden call with Netanyahu
item.description: In an interview with a US podcast, the US president says he was frustrated that Benjamin Netanyahu was "constantly fighting with Lebanon".

item.title: Rubio says concerns over Trump's health 'absurd and ridiculous'
item.description: The US president's mental acuity is in the spotlight, with the secretary of state shown videos of Donald Trump appearing to fall asleep during public appearances.

item.title: Music quiz: Can you pick these 10 classic Australian albums?
item.description: Take a closer look at some of the biggest albums in Australian music and tell us what you see.

item.title: After scooping the Oscars for Anora, Sean Baker brings his filmmaking tips to Sydney's Vivid Festival
item.description: From his humble beginnings to winning a bevy of big awards, including four Oscars, the filmmaker has forged a successful career by staying true to his guiding principles.

item.title: How to move forward from estrangement
item.description: Experts say healing might involve reducing emotional distress and gaining clarity from the situation.

item.title: AstraZeneca pulls critical breast cancer and endometriosis drug Zoladex from shelves
item.description: Patients have been left frustrated and scared after learning that a life-saving breast cancer and endometriosis drug is to be pulled from shelves in Australia and some other countries.

item.title: Falling house prices bring relief for some while a financial nightmare for others
item.description: A 46-year-old former "forever renter" believes her house is now worth less than what she paid for it and she is at risk of slipping into negative equity, which would mean her Marrickville home could be worth less than what she owes the bank.

item.title: Sportsbet owner ventures into giveaways with rewards club, anti-gambling advocates concerned
item.description: One of the world's biggest gambling companies is breaking into the 'rewards club' scene, raising the alarm among anti-gambling advocates.

item.title: Dive industry calls for more no-fish, no-drill zones as marine park review looms
item.description: Australia's dive industry has backed calls to expand no-fishing and no-drilling zones across the nation's oceans ahead of a federal review of marine parks.

item.title: Australia gains access to powerful AI amid questions about readiness
item.description: Fresh access to AI deemed too dangerous for public release has ramped up pressure on the government to respond to possible risks.

item.title: What do the pope and most Australians have in common? Neither trust AI companies
item.description: Most Australians appear to distrust and dislike artificial intelligence despite using technology more than people in other countries.

item.title: Who could replace Hobart's outgoing lord mayor Anna Reynolds?
item.description: After eight years in the top role, Anna Reynolds has announced she will not seek re-election in October. Her departure coincides with a notable reduction in the number of council representatives.

item.title: Australia's economic slowdown is just beginning, economists warn, as recession risks rise
item.description: The economy grew just 0.3 per cent in the first quarter, gross domestic product per capita went backwards, and interest rate rises and cost-of-living pressures are weighing on households.

item.title: Tasmania reconsiders bridge ban as Nuyina's refuelling saga drags on
item.description: Authorities are considering whether it is feasible for Australia's Antarctic icebreaker to safely travel under Hobart's Tasman Bridge, as the Tasmanian government tries to resolve a costly refuelling saga.

item.title: NSW government to introduce medicinal cannabis driving reforms
item.description: The Labor government plans to introduce a legal limit on the concentration of THC a driver can have in their system if they have a prescription.

item.title: Queensland beekeepers paying thousands each month to fend off varroa mites as colonies devastated
item.description: Beekeepers in Queensland say they are "barely hanging on" a year after the varroa mite was detected in the state for the first time.

item.title: Australia chasing men's rugby sevens World Championship at final tournament in Bordeaux
item.description: An unbeaten tournament victory in Spain gives Australia a chance of claiming the men's World Championship, with the title to be decided in this weekend's final round.

item.title: Puppy farm anger sees breeder withdraw proposal for facility with mandatory caesareans
item.description: An application for a proposed puppy farm in central Victoria has been withdrawn after lobbying by nearby residents and animal activists.

item.title: As Victor Wembanyama makes his NBA Finals debut, the league's new face is on its biggest stage
item.description: After years of searching, the NBA finally has its new face in Victor Wembanyama, and he is about to grace the league's biggest stage.

item.title: Extreme heatwaves have become a major threat to Australia's food supply, and not just in summer
item.description: Record-breaking heatwaves decimated crops in Australia this year and served as a "real wake-up call" for the urgent need to develop more heat-resistant crops and research into food security.

item.title: First Nations voters sent to back of queue for Voice election
item.description: South Australia's Electoral Commission was aware that forcing First Nations voters to queue twice for the Voice election would be perceived as "discriminatory behaviour". Despite that, voters say it still happened and, in some cases, they were turned away.

item.title: Leanne Castley is already partyless after quitting the Canberra Liberals but some want her gone entirely
item.description: Whether they're being driven away or choosing to jump in a getaway car, the women who have recently led the Canberra Liberals are no longer anywhere near the driver's seat.

item.title: Aryna Sabalenka 'just wants to quit tennis' after French Open quarterfinal loss to Diana Shnaider
item.description: Aryna Sabalenka feels the pain of a shock loss in the quarterfinals of the French Open, saying she just feels like smashing things.

item.title: Kakadu's Jim Jim and Twin Falls to stay closed throughout NT's peak tourism season
item.description: Parks Australia has announced popular Kakadu attractions Jim Jim Falls and Twin Falls will remain closed for the rest of the year, with one tourism operator saying the closures will lead to a "very hard" tourism season.

item.title: Mental health specialists from war-torn Ukraine turn to NSW town for trauma response strategies
item.description: Specialists from war-torn Ukraine are heading to regional New South Wales for trauma response and rehabilitation training.

item.title: Keir Starmer urges calm after protests over handcuffed Henry Nowak's death
item.description: Video of officers ignoring the pleas of a dying, innocent man has sparked a political storm about how police treat ‌different ethnicities.

item.title: Second Chance Choir helping people overcome hardship through music
item.description: In an unassuming building in Perth's north, the Second Chance Choir is bringing people together through music to help them put aside years of trauma, violence and substance abuse.

item.title: Matteo Berrettini withdraws injured at French Open as Matteo Arnaldi progresses to face Flavio Cobolli
item.description: Matteo Berrettini is once again forced out of a match by injury, retiring in the second set of his French Open quarterfinal with compatriot Matteo Arnaldi.

item.title: UN envoy warns Woodside's North West Shelf extension could violate human rights
item.description: An independent expert has urged Australia to "make sure that things are done properly", while Woodside pushed back against her involvement.

item.title: Israel and Lebanon agree to ceasefire but Hezbollah not involved in negotiations
item.description: Lebanon and Israel have agreed to a ceasefire, according to a joint statement with the United States.

item.title: Why TV renovation shows are a 'huge gamble' for their creators
item.description: A planned 12-month build that's still underway three years later is the running joke of design shows. But what happens when you're a TV producer trying to make a series of it?

item.title: Craig Foster concerned that FIFA World Cup players are not speaking out on US human rights record
item.description: Human rights advocate and former Socceroos captain Craig Foster says he is concerned by the lack of players speaking out about the United States' human rights record.

item.title: DRC's Ebola outbreak may have started six months ago, WHO chief says
item.description: Militant attacks have hampered efforts to combat the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda.

item.title: EPA asked to pause Karara magnetite mine assessment over water concerns
item.description: As a major miner seeks approval to extend its magnetite ore operations in regional Western Australia to 2048, shires are increasingly concerned about ensuring there's enough groundwater to go around.

item.title: US House passes measure to halt Iran war in rare rebuke of Trump
item.description: The US House of Representatives delivers President Donald Trump a rare rebuke, voting to halt military action in his war with Iran.

item.title: PM says Australia has 'ideological disagreement' with Trump administration after US reveals anti-slavery tariff
item.description: The planned new tariff is to be slapped on dozens of countries that have allegedly failed to take action to prevent slavery and forced labour.

item.title: Pentagon hires convicted January 6 rioter for national security job
item.description: The Trump administration has given a man who was jailed for his actions during the January 6, 2021, US Capitol riot a job in the Pentagon's policy office.

item.title: MP Andrew Wilkie uses parliament to reveal threats to NZYQ deportees in Nauru
item.description: A cohort of former detainees deported to Nauru by the federal government have faced threats of violence and verbal abuse, independent MP Andrew Wilkie has revealed under parliamentary privilege.

item.title: Crop failure fears as deadly bee parasite wipes out hives and forces beekeepers to quit
item.description: A new report warns of a massive pollination shortfall in Australia this year as varroa mite wipes out hives and forces beekeepers out.

item.title: Thomas Sewell was training at a Melbourne boxing gym. Now it's facing the consequences
item.description: ABC NEWS Verify can reveal that neo-Nazi Thomas Sewell has been training at a boxing gym in the outer-Melbourne suburb of Diamond Creek, a short drive from where he is living. The gym has been suspended by the sport's governing body.

item.title: Trump's tariff game has resumed and Australia could face some curveballs
item.description: Donald Trump had been using tariffs to increase his power over other countries until America's Supreme Court stepped in. He was never going to cede that power easily.

item.title: Chinese spies using LinkedIn to steal secrets from recruits, Five Eyes warns
item.description: Australia and its partners in the Five Eyes intelligence alliance warn that Chinese spies are using job platforms to post fake ads to lure recruits with access to sensitive information.

item.title: Oyster estuary becomes crown jewel after record breaking year
item.description: While oyster growers have had a tough time recently, one lake on the NSW Far South Coast is bucking the trend and setting new records along the way.

item.title: Beekeepers race to save hundreds of beehives after 'confronting' crash
item.description: A crew of nine beekeepers from across southern Queensland is rushing to save thousands of bees after the truck carrying their hives crashed on the Warrego Highway.

item.title: Pat Cummins hopeful of playing every Test of Australia's hectic 2026/27 schedule
item.description: Australian men's captain Pat Cummins says his body is feeling "awesome" ahead of a hectic 12-month stretch of Test cricket.

item.title: Four New Zealand MPs banned from China, Hong Kong and Macau after Taiwan trip
item.description: Beijing's move to ban four New Zealand MPs from travelling to China, Hong Kong and Macau is largely unprecedented, causing alarm in Canberra and Wellington.

item.title: Aged Care Minister Sam Rae grilled over human involvement in aged care assessments
item.description: Aged Care Minister Sam Rae has strongly defended an assessment tool for at-home funding support after ordering its urgent review.

item.title: Socceroos emerging star Lucas Herrington cruising into maiden World Cup
item.description: Lucas Herrington is on track to become Australia's youngest World Cup starter yet, but the 18-year-old is taking his incredible rise in his stride.

item.title: Lyell McEwin Hospital nurses walk off job for 24 hours amid pay dispute
item.description: Nurses and midwives at the Lyell McEwin Hospital have walked off the job as pay negotiations with the state government continue.

item.title: Greens question whether Jim Chalmers could exempt home owners in his electorate from CGT changes
item.description: While the party has indicated it is willing to help Labor pass the tax laws — which includes changes to negative gearing, capital gains tax (CGT) discount, trusts and the $250 tax offset — by early July, the Greens hold concerns about specifics in the bill.

item.title: Queensland minister Tim Nicholls 'acknowledges trauma' of Wolston Park survivors but does not apologise
item.description: Victims of alleged abuse at a Queensland mental health facility have been left disappointed after the state's health minister did not offer a public apology.

item.title: SA Budget 2026: Here are the winners and losers
item.description: South Australian Treasurer Tom Koutsantonis has unveiled the 2026-27 state budget. Here are the winners and losers.

item.title: EPA pushes back on need for real-time data on antibiotic use in Tasmania's salmon pens
item.description: The director of Tasmania's Environment Protection Authority rejects calls to release real-time information on the amount of antibiotics used by salmon producers, despite the companies already providing that information to other government bodies.

item.title: As Beijing cracks down on VPNs, internet users in China find ways around the Great Firewall
item.description: People in China say it is increasingly difficult to access the open internet, but they continue to find ways around Beijing's censorship apparatus.

item.title: Full Circle launches Adelaide musical theatre company with Catch Me If You Can
item.description: Musical theatre typically draws strong audiences in Adelaide where a troupe dedicated to the genre has just launched a professional company that will produce Catch Me If You Can in November.

item.title: 'Did he just swear?': Senate estimates rolls on, government under pressure
item.description: This week the Albanese government was under pressure on multiple fronts, Senate Estimates got a little sweary and Tony Abbott said he hadn't taken a "vow of silence".

item.title: Former Married at First Sight Australia bride Tahnee Cook says she felt pressure to be intimate on the show
item.description: Married At First Sight UK has become the centre of rape allegations. Now former MAFS Australia contestants and producers are speaking out about being coerced into intimacy.

item.title: Prosecutors argue bail should be refused for 'ISIS bride' fighting slavery charges
item.description: Prosecutors have argued Zeinab Ahmad actively participated in the mistreatment of a woman her family enslaved in Syria.

item.title: RBA not concerned about stagflation or wage-price spiral
item.description: RBA governor Michele Bullock says she is not concerned about stagflation or a wage-price spiral

item.title: Australian woman faces jail time over alleged cannabis vape liquid in Indonesia
item.description: The woman reportedly told officers she used the cannabis liquid to treat knee pain and depression, but authorities say she faces up to 20 years in jail and a hefty fine.

item.title: Souths stung by 'sticky spray' ruling as Manly continue Foran resurgence
item.description: The Manly Sea Eagles have provisionally moved into the NRL top four with a 28-14 win over the South Sydney Rabbitohs at Brookvale Oval.

item.title: Thursday finance with David Chau
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Coach defends doctors as Crows players play out game after head clash
item.description: Matthew Nicks defends his medical staff after two Crows were cleared to play out their win against the Cats without a concussion test after a nasty head clash.

item.title: WA 'spud king' Tony Galati's business fined $20k for unlicensed chip factory
item.description: Potato baron Tony Galati's business group has been fined $20,000 after it was found to have built an illegal Spuddies potato chip factory in WA's South West.

item.title: Advocates raise concerns over child safety inquiry's recommendations to elevate adoption in permanency hierarchy
item.description: Queensland's Commission of Inquiry into Child Safety report, released by the state government on Wednesday, found the availability of adoption should "not be limited by cultural background or ethnicity".

item.title: Government takes PFAS environmental contamination case to court
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Penny Wong says she believes activists who say they were sexually assaulted in Israeli custody
item.description: Foreign Minister Penny Wong has told a parliamentary hearing she believes the allegations of Australian activists, who say they were sexually assaulted while detained by Israeli forces.

item.title: Trump labels vote to curb Iran war powers 'unpatriotic' as Middle East ceasefire efforts continue
item.description: Donald Trump has condemned a House vote seeking to curb his war powers in Iran, as fresh fighting and diplomatic efforts unfold across the Middle East.

item.title: Ex-Trump adviser John Bolton to plead guilty to retaining classified information, sources say
item.description: The former national security adviser, who served in Donald Trump's first administration, is now a prominent critic of the president.

item.title: China blasts US over Tiananmen Square comments as families banned from visiting graves
item.description: As vigils to mark the 37th anniversary were held around the world, Chinese authorities reportedly banned families of those who died in 1989 from visiting their graves.

item.title: The best seasonal produce to look for in June
item.description: Winter has arrived. This is some of the best seasonal produce to look for.

item.title: Debuting at the Sydney Film Festival, Grace McKenzie's Yumburra is a lyrical ode to doing things differently
item.description: A slow-cinema treasure, Grace McKenzie's documentary, Yumburra, traces author Bruce Pascoe's establishment of a farm.

item.title: ABC news quiz: Mystery markings, a premature celebration and a fight over the Big … something
item.description: You too could learn the dangers of celebrating too soon if you underestimate this week's news quiz.

item.title: Fuel price expected to rise after government's excise cuts end on June 30
item.description: Experts say consumers have 30 days until they pay more at the pump if no peace deal is finalised in the Middle East and the Strait of Hormuz remains closed.

item.title: Rumours, rifts and a reported resignation form a murky picture of a war-ravaged Iran
item.description: A report about the resignation of Iran's president earlier this week came at a turbulent moment for Iran's regime, which is grappling with frictions between moderates and hardliners as the war drags on.

item.title: India's Great Nicobar Island Project goes ahead despite concerns about isolated Shompen tribe
item.description: India's Great Nicobar Island is home to one of the world's last isolated tribes who are believed to have lived there for thousands of years, but a rising China is set to see it undergo a major transformation at the Indian government's insistence.

item.title: Abnormal moles seen developing in patients chasing perfect suntan by using peptide melanotan-II
item.description: Skin specialists are sounding a warning about the potential cancer risks associated with unregulated peptides at the heart of a social media-driven beauty craze.

item.title: China warns Australia that 55 per cent beef tariff could apply within days
item.description: China is cracking down on beef imports and says it is ready to slap Australia with an additional 55 per cent tariff in the coming days.

item.title: Hanson prepares to take the big stage as Chalmers's budget backlash rolls on
item.description: While Labor is increasingly concerned about the long-term threat of One Nation, this week its attention was squarely on more immediate problems.

item.title: KPMG audits the RBA and runs its whistleblower hotline but maybe not for much longer
item.description: The Reserve Bank is distancing itself from KPMG with Governor Michele Bullock confirming the bank will re-tender its whistleblower hotline service.

item.title: Brumbies, Reds must create Super Rugby Pacific history in New Zealand to keep finals hopes alive
item.description: History is stacked against the Brumbies and Reds surviving week one of the Super Rugby Pacific finals, which kick off on Friday evening.

item.title: Australia's greenhouse gas emissions drop as renewable energy, batteries surge
item.description: Emissions are at their lowest point since the COVID pandemic shut down the economy, dropping 2.1 per cent over the year to December 2025.

item.title: RSV Nuyina can't access Hobart's marine refuelling depot, so what are its other options?
item.description: Australia's Antarctic icebreaker can't access the marine fuel depot near its home port in Hobart. It currently makes a 674 kilometre detour to Burnie, but are there other options?

item.title: Quit Victoria aims new campaign at vapers while warning laws fall short
item.description: Anti-vaping advocates have launched a new campaign warning young people of the health dangers posed by vaping. They say laws in Victoria are not helping their cause.

item.title: Farmer says flood plain harvesting has forced him to run cattle by the roadside
item.description: A western New South Wales farmer says dams built next door have stopped all water flows onto his property, forcing him to graze and water his stock by the side of the road.

item.title: One Nation cost former Queensland premier Rob Borbidge office. This is his warning for the current government
item.description: Former Nationals premier of Queensland Rob Borbidge says alarm bells are ringing within conservative ranks over surging support for One Nation, but he believes there is still time to prevent what some opinion polls suggest could be an electoral wipe-out.

item.title: Frustration over approach to dealing with Indian myna driving grassroots action
item.description: In one Queensland town, a wildlife carer is so concerned about the Indian myna's threat to native species, she has self-funded a bounty program that offers $5 for every bird humanely caught.

item.title: Public apology after Nepal's women's national team violates 'spirit of cricket'
item.description: Nepal's cricket board has offered a public apology after its women's team successfully had a batter timed out.

item.title: Fighting the spread of Ebola virus in one of the world's 'most volatile' regions
item.description: Tensions are running high as officials fight to stop the rapid spread of Ebola in the Democratic Republic of Congo, where aid workers tell the ABC they are already playing catch-up with the deadly disease.

item.title: Australia's Kaitlin Shave has 'unfinished business' after injury ended 2024 Paris Olympics debut
item.description: For an athlete still in the early stages of her professional career, Australian rugby 7s player Kaitlin Shave has already experienced the highs and lows of sport.

item.title: Federal government hopes bulk-billing contraceptive centres will empower more women
item.description: Misinformation, cost and a lack of trained providers have stopped many Australian women from accessing what doctors consider the most effective form of reversible contraception.

item.title: No surprises but risks aplenty in Tom Koutsantonis's comeback budget
item.description: Some things haven't changed since Labor Treasurer Tom Koutsantonis last handed down a state budget in 2017.

item.title: Broken Hill pays homage to champion of outback's Silver City Cinema
item.description: The Silver City Cinema in remote NSW has stood the test of time for nearly 60 years. Now its new owners want to honour the legend who kept it alive.

item.title: Hezbollah rejects Lebanon ceasefire as Israeli strikes kill four
item.description: Iran has made a ceasefire in Lebanon a condition for any peace deal with Washington, and has suggested in recent days that it could intervene directly if Israel keeps up attacks there.

item.title: Member for Lingiari Marion Scrymgour sat down for an exclusive interview with Stateline NT in Alice Springs.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Light shed on how Kumanjayi Little Baby ended up in a town camp in Alice Springs the night she disappeared
item.description: Member for Lingiari Marion Scrymgour says the mother of Kumanjayi Little Baby had been living in insecure housing in Alice Springs so she could access specialist support for her daughter, before the five-year-old's tragic death in April.

item.title: Australia loses third ODI to drop series in Pakistan after another poor batting display in Lahore
item.description: Pakistan comfortably chases down 158 to win the third one-day international in Lahore, beating Australia 2-1 in their series.

item.title: WA's new Chief Health Officer Clare Huppatz confident state's Ebola risk is low
item.description: Clare Huppatz, the first woman to be appointed WA's chief health officer, takes up her role at a time when Ebola, diphtheria and the flu are public health concerns.

item.title: Nauiyu leaders race to bring services back online as first residents return to flood-ravaged community
item.description: Residents of the Northern Territory town of Nauiyu / Daly River have started returning home after spending three months in evacuation centres following extensive flooding.

item.title: Mirra Andreeva to face Polish qualifier Maja Chwalińska in women's French Open final
item.description: Nineteen-year-old Mirra Andreeva reaches her first grand slam final at the French Open where she will take on Poland's Maja Chwalińska, the first qualifier to reach the Paris decider in the Open Era.

item.title: Ukrainian strikes on Russian patrol ship in Crimea
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Volodomyr Zelenskyy writes open letter inviting Vladimir Putin to direct negotiations to end Ukraine war
item.description: An open letter is the first public message Mr Zelenskyy has written directly to Mr Putin since Russia launched its full-scale invasion in 2022.

item.title: ASIC has 8 contracts with KPMG despite audit scandal, ASX loses ground — as it happened
item.description: ASIC tells Senate estimates it has eight active contracts with KPMG, despite the company facing an audit scandal. Meanwhile, the ASX lost ground. Catch up on the day's events and insights in our live markets blog.

item.title: Australian bull semen exports to China resume after pandemic pause
item.description: After a five year break, livestock farmers in China will use Australian genetics to produce premium beef for traditional dining and hot pot markets.

item.title: 100,000 exotic cockroaches seized from alleged Bathurst breeder
item.description: More than 100,000 live exotic cockroaches have allegedly been seized from a commercial breeder in Central West NSW.

item.title: Authorities investigating after dozens of birds found dead or dying at Coorow in WA's Wheatbelt
item.description: Authorities will investigate why 78 birds were found dead or dying in a wheatbelt town, with locals speculating the cause could be related to mouse baits.

item.title: Victoria Police chief Mike Bush says firebombings will stop when police work their way to 'top of the tree'
item.description: Criminals in the construction sector, youth offending and station staffing are discussed in a wide-ranging interview with Victoria Police Chief Commissioner Mike Bush.

item.title: Aiden O'Neill fully fit for Socceroos' FIFA World Cup campaign after ankle injury scare
item.description: Aiden O'Neill put a scare through the Socceroos camp when he went down with an ankle injury in early May, but says he is fit and ready for his World Cup debut.

item.title: Finished Off Campus and need to keep that fire burning? Here's what to watch next
item.description: Hannah and Garrett's steamy love story might be on ice, but we've found more romance and yearning to tick your comfort-viewing boxes.

item.title: Five countries elected to UN Security Council as Germany misses out
item.description: Portugal and Austria have defeated Germany for seats on the powerful but deeply divided UN Security Council in a hotly contested race after intense campaigning.

item.title: Public servant claims SA government has threatened him with $4 million bill in golf course fight
item.description: A South Australian public servant taking the state government to court over a controversial redevelopment in the Adelaide Parklands says he will continue to fight the case despite it potentially leading to him losing his house or becoming bankrupt.

item.title: Matildas star Mary Fowler reflects on FA Cup and WSL win with Manchester City
item.description: Sport news, live scores, features, analysis and photos from the world of sport. Comprehensive coverage of AFL, cricket, football, rugby league and rugby union. Audio interviews. News stories and highlights in video. Sports news bulletin.

item.title: ABC Classic 100: Greatest of All Time — when it happens, how to listen and more
item.description: Australia's favourite classical music poll is back for another year — here's when it happens, how to listen and details about the ABC Classic 100 in Concert.

item.title: Government accused of misleading public on NDIS cuts in heated Senate hearing
item.description: The federal government has been accused of selling its cuts to the NDIS as a crackdown on fraud, while instead pursuing billions of dollars of dramatic savings by slashing participant packages.

item.title: NRL heavyweight Melbourne Storm in 'positive’ talks with Tasmanian government to bring games south
item.description: Melbourne Storm chief executive Justin Rodski says he's "got no doubt" a deal with Tasmania can be reached if the commercial interests line up, following a round of courting from premier Jeremy Rockliff.

item.title: Israel's military crossed a tank over a major Lebanese river. We found where
item.description: Images of the first tanks to roll over Lebanon's Litani River were posted online this week. ABC NEWS Verify has found the spot where Israel's military crossed.

item.title: Ash Gardner shines for Australia in ICC T20 Women's World Cup warm up
item.description: Ash Gardner will hit Australia's Twenty20 World Cup warm-up matches full of confidence after a half-century in the last of three informal hit-outs against South Africa.

item.title: TGA still investigating sunscreens that Choice testing found failed to meet SPF claims
item.description: Most of the sunscreens that failed to meet their SPF label claims, according to independent testing, are still under investigation by the regulator one year later.

item.title: Major fishing competition the Barra Nationals gets under way after six-week delay due to flooding
item.description: The country's biggest annual barramundi fishing competition is under way on the Northern Territory's remote Daly River, after the tournament was delayed by six weeks due to extensive flooding.

item.title: Princess of Wales hugs cancer patient who has finished treatment
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: In pictures: Princess Catherine shares emotional moment with cancer patient
item.description: The Princess of Wales joined in a hospital cancer ward celebration for a young mum who completed chemotherapy.

item.title: What to know about the New World screwworm fly and its reappearance in the US
item.description: The New World screwworm fly is threatening the $US113 billion United States cattle industry for the first time in more than a half century.

item.title: Oympic prize money debate continues after Enhanced Games incentives
item.description: Australian swimmer Shayna Jack has added her voice to growing calls for Olympic prize money, believing athletes are missing out on what they financially deserve.

item.title: Marjane Satrapi, Iranian-French cartoonist and filmmaker known for Persepolis, 'dies of sadness' at the age of 56
item.description: A committed advocate to furthering free speech and women's rights in her birth country, Satrapi lost her husband, Swedish actor Mattias Ripa, in April last year, and "died of sadness", according to French media.

item.title: Kitten left for dead after being thrown from car finds new home with rescuers
item.description: A kitten left for dead after being thrown from a moving car has spent months recovering and is now at a new home with two familiar faces.

item.title: Matildas star Mary Fowler wants to make an impact and have fun along the way
item.description: Matildas star Mary Fowler is using her platform to share what matters to her, as she navigates becoming a truly global footballing superstar.

item.title: Teen who allegedly stormed plane at Avalon Airport with shotgun to face adult court
item.description: A Victorian teenager who allegedly tried to hijack a plane has failed in a bid to keep his case in the Children's Court, where the maximum penalty is four years in jail.

item.title: Former ACT Liberal politician Gary Humphries speaks to ABC Stateline
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Energy prices to fall across Queensland after national drop in prices
item.description: Household power bills will drop by about 7 per cent, while business bills will drop by 8 per cent from July 1.

item.title: NSW Police officer Benedict Bryant sentenced over death of Indigenous teenager Jai Wright
item.description: A NSW Police officer convicted over a fatal collision that killed an Indigenous teenager has avoided time behind bars.

item.title: New Zealand soccer player Tim Payne meets social media influencer who made him a celebrity
item.description: New Zealand soccer player Tim Payne has met social media influencer Valen Scarsini after the Argentine encouraged his fans to follow the unheralded All Whites player.

item.title: 100,000 exotic cockroaches allegedly seized from commercial breeder
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Hidden cosmic web revealed by WA radio telescope with biggest-ever magnetic map
item.description: Data gathered by scientists at a radio observatory in WA's Murchison has resulted in a new "map" detailing the magnetic web of our observable universe.

item.title: Hollywood actor James Handy stabbed to death in front yard at age 81
item.description: James Handy appeared in almost 150 films or TV series, according to his IMDb page, with his last role being a bartender in Top Gun: Maverick.

item.title: Future of iconic wine label Wolf Blass unclear amid Treasury Wine Estates restructure
item.description: A cloud hangs over the future of the famous Barossa brand as its maker, Treasury Wine Estates, looks to cull more than half of its labels.

item.title: Four Australians in the hunt after Women's US Open first round in LA
item.description: Four of the six Australian golfers competing at the 81st Women's US Open are within sight of the leader after the opening round in Los Angeles.

item.title: Exploring the link between gardening and better mental health
item.description: While gardening alone is not a treatment for mental illness, a growing body of evidence links it with higher levels of mental wellbeing and life satisfaction.

item.title: Recorded flu numbers drop drastically but expert warns caution
item.description: Flu cases have come down since the record-breaking 2025 season but experts warn against a "false sense of security".

item.title: Please explain: One Nation housing policy confuses its own as senator's interview turns into a 'train wreck'
item.description: After Barnaby Joyce needed a do-over interview to explain One Nation's housing policy, a senator's interview was terminated after it turned into a 'train wreck'.

item.title: Police manhunt under way in Darwin for 44yo bikie Brandt Graham after escape from court custody
item.description: A manhunt is under way in Darwin as police search for Brandt Graham, a 44-year-old Mongols bikie who scaled an enclosed courthouse dock and escaped from custody today.

item.title: Brandt Graham escaping from court
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Mona visitor numbers are up, but gambling continues to prop up loss-making Tasmanian gallery
item.description: Newly filed accounts give a first glimpse of Mona's ultimate holding company, showing the loss-making gallery remains heavily reliant on gambling funds.

item.title: Albanese admits One Nation's rise partly why he moved to change negative gearing and capital gains tax
item.description: The rise of One Nation is partly behind the government's decision to take on tax reform in this year's budget, the prime minister has admitted.

item.title: Body-in-suitcase double murderer's appeal to reduce prison time dismissed by NSW court
item.description: The NSW Court of Criminal Appeal dismisses a bid by Daniel Holdom to have his sentence reduced after he pleaded guilty in 2018 to murdering his girlfriend and her two-year-old daughter before dumping the child's body in a suitcase on the side of a remote highway.

item.title: Sunshine Coast woman appeals conviction for arranging woodchipper murder of ex-boyfriend
item.description: Sharon Graham was found guilty in 2023 of masterminding 54-year-old Bruce Sauders' murder, in an elaborate plan to get almost $1 million worth of his life insurance, superannuation and assets.

item.title: ASIC names two KPMG partners it is formally investigating over audit scandal
item.description: ASIC tells a Senate estimates committee it is formally investigating KPMG and that it still has eight current contracts with the firm.

item.title: City of Parramatta Council CEO sent anonymous email alleging 'systemic corruption', ICAC hearing told
item.description: The head of a Western Sydney council sent an anonymous email to reporters alleging "systemic corruption" by a senior staff member was being "swept under the rug".

item.title: Shattered Storm superstar to miss entire NRL season
item.description: Melbourne will be without Queensland Origin player Xavier Coates for the rest of the NRL season after the winger ruptured his Achilles at training on Friday.

item.title: Boxer Andrew Moloney ready for IBF title fight against champion Willibaldo Garcia
item.description: A sense of calm has swept over Andrew Moloney since arriving in Japan for a world title shot built on years, and hundreds of thousands of dollars, of sacrifice.

item.title: Tasmanian coroner finds doctor's prescribing played a direct role in two deaths
item.description: A coroner has found a doctor's prescription of drugs to two drug-dependent patients had a "direct causative role" in their deaths. Their report says the doctor had a "permissive approach to opioid prescribing" for more than two decades.

item.title: Anthony Monteleone jailed for stabbing former girlfriend at gym car park in Sydney's Alexandria
item.description: A judge said Anthony Monteleone was "utterly delusional" in repeated messages to the woman before attacking her outside a Sydney gym in 2024.

item.title: GST push to follow SA budget, treasurer says — but opposition wants it spent on debt
item.description: South Australia's treasurer has foreshadowed a push to secure GST revenue, calling on MPs across the political spectrum to step up their advocacy efforts — but the opposition has questioned why a recent GST windfall is not being used to counter soaring debt.

item.title: Bulldogs defender calls out abusive fan after fiery clash with Hawks star
item.description: Michael Sellwood has the last laugh against opponent Nick Watson and a spectator, as the Western Bulldogs toppled Hawthorn in another tense finish.

item.title: ANU governance issues cost $100m in reputational damage, interim vice-chancellor says
item.description: The interim vice-chancellor of the Australian National University says a string of governance problems has cost the university about $100 million in reputational damage.

item.title: WA man charged over alleged offensive emails to federal parliamentarian
item.description: The Australian Federal Police are linking a 70-year-old man to emails sent to an electorate office after an investigation that began in April.

item.title: NT coroner hands down findings into 3yo Mitchell Thomas's tragic death in Alice Springs house fire
item.description: A "heroic" Alice Springs police officer who risked his life repeatedly entering a burning building in trying to rescue a three-year-old boy will be awarded a medal of valour.

item.title: ACT Brumbies knocked out of Super Rugby Pacific finals by dominant Hurricanes
item.description: The ACT Brumbies have been dumped out of the Super Rugby Pacific finals series after setting unwanted club records in Wellington on Friday.

item.title: Friday Finance with David Chau
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Lawyer says NSW woman ignorant of alleged plot to smuggle fugitive Qld bikie overseas from WA
item.description: Fatma Skaf faced the Karratha Magistrates Court today over her alleged involvement in a plot to spirit Queensland bikie Dylan Cvek out of the country on a luxury yacht.

item.title: Chinese President Xi Jinping will travel to North Korea next week in first visit since 2019
item.description: Kim Jong Un has been trying to improve ties with China, North Korea's biggest trading partner and provider of aid.

item.title: Andrew made money subletting cottages on his almost rent-free estate, watchdog finds
item.description: Former prince Andrew pocketed income from cottages on the Windsor estate while living at Royal Lodge under a lease that required virtually no rent, a watchdog report has found.

item.title: Another pandemic 'something we need to prepare for', WA's public health chief says
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Putin rejects offer to meet Zelenskyy as European leaders warn Ukraine war poses 'direct threat' to nearby nations
item.description: A Ukrainian naval drone has exploded in Romania's Black Sea port of Constanta, as European leaders warned the war in Ukraine is becoming a "direct threat" to countries along the EU's eastern border.

item.title: Matt Corby on new album Tragic Magic, returning to live TV and writing Brother
item.description: He got his start as a singing TV idol, but Matt Corby is making music for himself now, finding the magic in grief, growth and family.

item.title: ABC 50/50 quiz: Testing your knowledge of a 60s rock classic, a famous poem and Euphoria vs Hacks
item.description: Each question in the ABC 50/50 quiz has only two options: one is right; the other is wrong. But there's a catch.

item.title: Zoe Pepper and Fiona Wright take on the housing crisis in Birthright and Kill Your Boomers
item.description: Zoe Pepper and Fiona Wright are among the millennial artists using their work to tackle the housing crisis, which has seen house prices rise by 400 per cent in 25 years.

item.title: Buyer's agency Dashdot's collapse leaves hundreds of customers tens of thousands out of pocket
item.description: Liquidators say at least 700 Dashdot clients could be caught up in the buyer's agency collapse, with some who paid large up-front fees fearing they may not get their money back.

item.title: Medical technology sector raises fresh concerns with Labor’s capital gains tax overhaul
item.description: Medical technology companies say new restrictions on tax refunds for research and development will hurt health startups in Australia.

item.title: CCTV captures moment military officers throwing acid over man
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Lawyer of Indonesian man attacked with acid says trial of perpetrators is a 'sham'
item.description: Four military officers are facing trial over an acid attack on an Indonesian human rights campaigner that left him seriously disfigured. But some lawyers say the legal process is a "sham".

item.title: Aerial video of Silverwater correctional centre
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: The last holding place
item.description: When Tammy Shipley felt herself sliding into psychosis, she made a heartbreaking decision: She walked into a police station and asked to be arrested, believing prison would keep her safe. Here's what happened next.

item.title: 'Volcano on the sea floor': Labor is aware of the challenges posed by One Nation, budget
item.description: This week the Coalition's weakness in Question Time reminded onlookers of Albanese's command of the Westminster system, while Labor insiders expressed their anxieties about One Nation's polling surge.

item.title: Tap-and-go technology to launch across Melbourne train network after many months of delays
item.description: Melbourne commuters will be able to use their credit card, phone or smartwatch across the train network this month, joining other major cities with ticketless travel after repeated delays.

item.title: Mates team up with NSW Riverina farmers to help feed people in Sudan
item.description: An unlikely pair from the NSW Riverina plan to ship 100 tonnes of wheat to war-torn Sudan.

item.title: British actor Anthony Head, known for Buffy the Vampire Slayer and Ted Lasso, dies aged 72
item.description: The British actor was well known for roles in Buffy the Vampire Slayer and Ted Lasso.

item.title: Fake police scam targets Chinese diaspora with threats and forged documents
item.description: Justin thought he was being investigated by the Chinese police, but was pulled into a vicious and frightening scam targeting the Chinese diaspora all over the world.

item.title: Alexander Zverev beats Jakub Menšík to reach French Open final and Flavio Cobolli advances
item.description: Alexander Zverev moves to within touching distance of a long-coveted major title, while Flavio Cobolli reaches the final after Matteo Arnaldi withdraws due to illness.

item.title: Isolated showers expected to return to parts of Queensland this weekend
item.description: Settled, clear and cool conditions will continue into Queensland's first weekend of winter, with showers brewing from late Sunday.
